steps.title: Identify the two clauses
steps.content:
• Choice A — parks and: "and" is a coordinating conjunction, but when joining two longer main clauses it conventionally requires a comma before it — "parks, and it prioritized..." Without the comma, this is non-standard. ✗
  • Choice B — parks: No punctuation at all between the two main clauses creates a run-on sentence. ✗
  • Choice C — parks;: A semicolon correctly joins two independent clauses without a conjunction. Both sides are complete sentences, so this is grammatically sound. ✓
  • Choice D — parks,: A comma alone between two main clauses with no conjunction creates a comma splice. ✗

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: A semicolon is the only option that correctly separates two independent clauses without requiring a conjunction. That confirms C is correct.
